# RustyNES v1.10.0 — "Arcade" (Libretro core + dependency refresh)

The **native Libretro core release**: `crates/rustynes-libretro` builds the `rustynes_libretro` shared library (`.so` / `.dylib` / `.dll` by platform) that plugs the byte-identical, cycle-accurate RustyNES engine straight into **RetroArch** and every other libretro frontend — bringing the fourth platform surface (after desktop, Android, and iOS) onto the exact same deterministic core. **Arcade** also refreshes the desktop dependency tier (egui 0.34.3 → 0.35.0 plus an in-constraint transitive sweep) and fixes the iOS release-workflow signing-secret gate. Everything is **additive / host-only**: the deterministic `#![no_std]` chip stack is untouched, the shipped / native / `no_std` / wasm builds stay **byte-identical**, **AccuracyCoin holds 100% (139/139)**, and `nestest` is 0-diff. This is the **last planned feature release before the v2.0.0 "Timebase" core rewrite** (ADR 0002).

---

## The Libretro core (`rustynes-libretro`)

A complete, native libretro implementation over the `rust-libretro` FFI wrapper (`crate-type = ["cdylib", "staticlib"]`), depending on `rustynes-core` alone — no frontend code is shared or duplicated.

- **In-memory ROM loading** — `need_fullpath = false` with `RETRO_ENVIRONMENT_GET_GAME_INFO_EXT`: the frontend hands the ROM as a RAM buffer and the core calls `rustynes_core::Nes::from_rom(data)` directly, so the core side needs no `std::fs` at all. A malformed header (`RomError`) returns `false` to the frontend gracefully — never a crash.
- **Allocation-free video** — the PPU's native 256×240 RGBA8 framebuffer is swizzled per-pixel (`chunk.swap(0, 2)`) into the negotiated **XRGB8888** format and dispatched with the exact 1024-byte pitch; no per-frame allocation, no dynamic geometry.
- **Batched stereo audio** — the core's `f32` synthesis output is scaled to interleaved signed 16-bit stereo and emitted in a single `audio_sample_batch` per frame, riding the frontend's dynamic-rate resampler for perfect-pitch sync.
- **RetroAchievements memory maps** — `RETRO_MEMORY_SYSTEM_RAM` (WRAM), `RETRO_MEMORY_SAVE_RAM` (SRAM, exposed via the core's `sram()` accessor for battery persistence), and `RETRO_MEMORY_VIDEO_RAM` are mapped for RetroArch's zero-cost achievement scanning.
- **Deterministic save states** — `get_serialize_size` / `on_serialize` / `on_unserialize` ride the core's deterministic snapshot machinery, which makes the core **rollback-netplay (GGPO) ready** out of the box.
- **Buildbot-compatible cross-compilation** — the crate `Makefile` translates the libretro buildbot's `platform` / `ARCH` variables into Rust target triples (unix / osx / win / android), staged for the upstream `libretro-super` fork workflow documented in [`docs/libretro/UPSTREAM_SYNC.md`](https://github.com/doublegate/RustyNES/blob/main/docs/libretro/UPSTREAM_SYNC.md).
- **Documentation** — architecture, walkthrough, implementation specifics, and advanced-features docs land under [`docs/libretro/`](https://github.com/doublegate/RustyNES/tree/main/docs/libretro), with the plan history in `to-dos/libretro/`.

Every `unsafe` FFI block carries a `// SAFETY:` invariant (frontend-owned pointers, validity windows), matching the project's confined-unsafe rule.

## Dependency refresh

- **egui GUI stack 0.34.3 → 0.35.0** (consolidated Dependabot #211 / #212 / #213 in PR #215): `egui`, `egui-wgpu`, and `egui-winit` move as one lockstep set. The 0.35 tier lands entirely within the existing render / windowing stack — `egui-wgpu 0.35` pins `wgpu ^29.0`, `egui-winit 0.35` pins `winit ^0.30.13`, `egui 0.35` pins `accesskit ^0.24.1`, all already resolved — so there is **no wgpu / winit / accesskit / naga movement**. The one API break (`Panel::show_inside` → the renamed `Panel::show(ui, ..)`) was migrated across the three shell / debugger call sites.
- **In-constraint transitive sweep** — ~20 SemVer-compatible patch/minor bumps (`anyhow 1.0.103`, `clap_complete 4.6.7`, `time 0.3.53`, `uuid 1.23.4`, `js-sys` / `web-sys 0.3.103`, `wasm-bindgen 0.2.126`, …), with the `wasm-bindgen` CLI pin in `web/Trunk.toml` bumped in lockstep (the library and CLI version-check each other, and a mismatch fails the GitHub Pages deploy).
- **Deliberately deferred** as out-of-tier or breaking: `wgpu 30` / `naga 30` (would break the egui-wgpu 0.35 render tier), `getrandom 0.4` (breaking wasm API), and `uniffi 0.32` (the mobile bridge needs its own Kotlin/Swift regeneration + on-device pass).

## Fixed

- **iOS release workflow no longer fails on every `v*` tag push** — a "Detect iOS signing secrets" step now gates the fastlane install + TestFlight upload: with the App Store Connect / fastlane `match` secrets un-provisioned the upload is skipped with a green notice instead of a red `OpenSSL::PKey::EC` abort; once provisioned, the upload runs automatically. xcframework build validation is unchanged.

## Quality & determinism

**AccuracyCoin 100.00% (139/139)**, `nestest` 0-diff against the Nintendulator golden log, the full CI gate matrix green (fmt, the complete clippy feature matrix at `-D warnings`, `RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings` rustdoc, the `no_std` chip-stack cross-compile, both wasm clippy lines, and the `test-roms` accuracy suite). The libretro core consumes only existing `rustynes-core` public APIs, so the desktop / Android / iOS / wasm builds are byte-identical to v1.9.9 and the determinism contract (same seed + ROM + input ⇒ bit-identical framebuffer and audio) holds everywhere — including across the new RetroArch surface.

## What's next

**v2.0.0 "Timebase"** — the integer / fractional master-clock timebase refactor (ADR 0002): the one-clock + every-cycle-bus-access collapse (Mesen2-style fractional master clock with a φ1/φ2 access split) that is the documented prerequisite for closing the remaining hard-tier accuracy residuals, and the one release expected to break byte-identity / save-state compatibility. After Timebase, the **v2.0.1 → v2.1.0** mobile-finalization train re-ports Android (v2.0.1–v2.0.4) and iOS (v2.0.5–v2.0.8) onto the new core (v2.0.9 = dual-app readiness) and launches them jointly — Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid — at **v2.1.0**.

## Install

Desktop binaries for Linux (x86_64), macOS (aarch64), and Windows (x86_64) are attached below; the web build is live at <https://doublegate.github.io/RustyNES/>. To build the libretro core locally: `cargo build --release -p rustynes-libretro` (or drive the crate `Makefile` with the standard libretro `platform` / `ARCH` variables), then load the resulting `rustynes_libretro` shared library in RetroArch via *Load Core*. See [`docs/libretro/architecture.md`](https://github.com/doublegate/RustyNES/blob/main/docs/libretro/architecture.md) for the design, [`docs/libretro/WALKTHROUGH.md`](https://github.com/doublegate/RustyNES/blob/main/docs/libretro/WALKTHROUGH.md) for the setup guide, and [`docs/libretro/UPSTREAM_SYNC.md`](https://github.com/doublegate/RustyNES/blob/main/docs/libretro/UPSTREAM_SYNC.md) for the libretro-super / libretro-docs upstream workflow. Dual-licensed MIT OR Apache-2.0.

Built on the **v1.9.x "Sunrise"** iOS line, the **v1.8.x "Android"** line, the writable + programmable **v1.7.0 "Forge"** line, and the cycle-accurate v1.0.0 production core.
